Ad Text:
HRD Employer of Choice, Accredited Family Inclusive Workplace Join a forward-thinking organisation that values new ideas and innovation

Join a nationally recognised team with strong Cisco and Splunk partnerships

Location Sydney, Brisbane, Canberra or Melbourne




This is an opportunity to join a highly regarded national technology team delivering complex Cisco solutions for customers across a broad range of industries.

Working alongside experienced architects, sales specialists and delivery teams, you’ll play a hands-on role in designing, implementing and optimising analytics and network intelligence solutions that solve real business challenges.




In this role you will: 

Design and deliver Cisco Splunk solutions across customer environments

Lead or support end-to-end implementations, including discovery, configuration and deployment activities

Partner with customers to understand technical requirements and recommend fit-for-purpose solutions

Contribute to solution development, proposals and technical documentation

Collaborate closely with sales, pre-sales and vendor stakeholders on joint opportunities

Provide technical guidance and best practice advice across Splunk and related technologies

Support go-to-market initiatives, enablement activities and ongoing solution improvement 

  

About You 

Strong hands-on experience designing and implementing Splunk solutions, ideally with advanced Cisco Networking, Security, or Data Centre knowledge also

Demonstrated capability working across infrastructure, security or observability environments

A strong desire to learn, or experience with, agentic software development

Able to translate technical concepts into practical customer outcomes

Comfortable engaging with both technical and non-technical stakeholders

Experience supporting solution scoping, technical workshops and delivery activities

Strong troubleshooting, critical thinking and problem-solving skills

Background in consulting, professional services or technical project delivery environments




If you are looking for a role where you can combine deep technical capability with meaningful customer impact, while working alongside some of the industry’s leading cloud, security and data specialists, we’d love to hear from you.

 

Who is Data#3? 

Data#3 is ASX200 listed and reported gross sales of $3 billion in FY25. Headquartered in Brisbane we have approximately 1,400 employees across 12 locations in Australia and Fiji.  




We have been voted HRD's Employer of Choice for the last 10 years and named one of Australia’s Best Workplaces in Technology by Great Place To Work® in 2024 




At Data#3, we value diversity, equity, and inclusion as key to a thriving workplace. We are committed to building a culture where everyone feels welcome, respected, and valued—and we encourage your application. 




Data#3 will request as part of the recruitment process, reference checks, police checks and verification of your right to work in Australia.

Senior Manager – Analytics & AI
Exclusive Opportunity | Greenfield Projects

The business is investing heavily in modernising its analytics environment across APAC and is implementing Microsoft Fabric as its global enterprise data platform standard. With large volumes of data across Salesforce, ERP, operational systems and customer platforms, this role will play a key part in building trusted reporting, improving governance and enabling scalable AI adoption.

This is not a deeply hands-on engineering role and the focus is on platform ownership, governance, stakeholder engagement, prioritisation and delivery oversight while working closely with Microsoft partners and senior business leaders. The environment is still relatively early in its data maturity journey, making this a genuine greenfield opportunity to shape enterprise reporting, governance standards and future AI capability from the ground up.

You’ll lead initiatives across:

Microsoft Fabric platform roll-out and optimisation.
Vendor and Microsoft partner management.
Data governance, stewardship and data dictionaries.
Enterprise reporting and cross-platform analytics uplift.
AI project intake, prioritisation and business case evaluation.
Leadership reporting and AI pipeline reviews.
Global AI policy and governance alignment.
Cost management across platform implementation and ongoing usage.
Greenfield data, analytics and AI initiatives across the organisation.
About You
8+ years’ experience across Data & Analytics, BI or consulting environments.
Strong understanding of Microsoft Fabric, Co Pilot, Power BI and modern data platforms.
Experience uplifting reporting maturity and governance frameworks.
Strong stakeholder engagement and vendor management capability.
Commercial mindset with exposure to budgeting, prioritisation and delivery outcomes.
Exposure to AI governance, enablement or business-led AI initiatives highly regarded.
Comfortable operating in evolving or low-maturity environments.
Why Apply?
High-impact greenfield opportunity.
Strong executive visibility and influence.
Significant investment in Microsoft Fabric and AI capability.
Opportunity to shape long-term enterprise data strategy.
Collaborative environment with strong growth potential.
Sydney-based candidates with full Australian working rights preferred.

Are you a passionate leader focused on driving digital transformation and integrating cutting-edge capabilities into a dynamic retail environment? As our Digital Transformation Leader (Country Digital Market Leader), you will play a critical role in shaping IKEA Australia & New Zealand's digital future, working to maximise business value and enhance experiences for both our co-workers and customers. 

About Digital & Expansion

IKEA Retail, as the core business of Ingka, is dedicated to creating a better everyday life for many by ensuring long-term profitable growth, making IKEA more affordable, accessible, and sustainable. This commitment involves leading retail operations, serving happy customers, and developing IKEA as a purpose-led brand, while continuously expanding our presence to meet customers wherever and however they choose. We proactively set the digital agenda, driving the rapid development and operation of digital products and services using new technologies and agile methods, thereby fostering the continuous growth of our technical, people, and business skills to enhance our digital capabilities.

As the Digital Transformation Leader at IKEA Australia & New Zealand, you will guide and execute market-level digitalisation, seamlessly integrating digital capabilities into daily operations. You will collaborate closely with the Market Manager/s and units’ management teams to support and coordinate the digital agenda, maximising business value. This role is crucial in fostering a global environment where Ingka’s digitalisation capabilities and culture thrive across countries. Your responsibilities will include but are not limited to:

Acting as a Digital Business Partner to the Market Manager and unit’s management team, ensuring value realisation by leading market digitalisation efforts and delivering tactical implementations aligned with business needs to secure a future-proof digital market.

Supporting the building of local competence and performance in digital by setting relevant goals collaboratively with market management and creating a robust digital network within the market to accelerate digitalisation.

Driving the digital experience at the market level for both co-workers and customers, leveraging data insights. You will work with the Business Navigation Manager and Country Digital Operations leader to secure the correct budget framework to consciously improve our digital experience.

Identifying digital-related challenges and opportunities within units and proactively driving fit-for-purpose technological solutions, ensuring issues are resolved in a way that strengthens operational performance, supports co-workers, and enhances the overall digital maturity of the market.

Working closely within a networked organisation across the Ingka world, collaborating with other Digital Market Leaders to share best practices, success stories, and learnings to help our entire community strive for excellence.

About you

Your expertise in digital systems allows you to understand how technology impacts the business, coupled with a proven ability to deliver digital projects and manage planning effectively. You possess good knowledge of leading change processes at all organisational levels and are adept at continuous improvement within the omni-retail business. Your understanding of consumer, customer, and co-worker needs is comprehensive, complemented by good knowledge of budget and service management. You demonstrate a strong ability to lead in complex, ever-changing landscapes with multiple priorities, always keeping the business, co-worker, and customer at heart. With excellent communication and strong people skills, you excel at building relationships, influencing, and inspiring leaders and co-workers. You can effectively drive positive change to achieve business goals, approach digitalisation holistically to link it to value creation, and skilfully translate technical language into business language to foster understanding and motivate teams towards common goals. To be considered for this role you will have:

A university degree IT or Business or Project Management and a minimum of 5 years of related experience.

Proven experience in the omni-retail business.

Proven experience in driving business improvements through digitalisation.




Additional information:

Permanent, full-time, 76hrs/fortnight.

The location for the role is Service Office, Tempe NSW, with a minimum of 3 days a week on-site.

Internal role title is Country Digital Market Leader.

If you are successful for this role, you will be required to obtain a national police check.

Job Title: General Manager - Data, AI and Cyber
Ad Text:
Leadership opportunity
Enterprise organisation
Competitive Salary
 
We are partnering with a well-established national organisation seeking a modern technology leader to drive the next evolution of its enterprise data, AI and cyber capability.
  
This role will lead an established data and analytics function while helping shape how the organisation adopts AI, strengthens governance and continues building a secure, future-focused technology environment.
  
We are looking for someone who combines strong enterprise data leadership experience with a genuine background in cyber security earlier in their career, someone who understands both innovation and protection, and how to balance the two without slowing the business down.
  
While this is not a deeply hands-on technical role, credibility matters. The successful candidate will need to bring enough practical experience across data, cyber and enterprise technology environments to lead teams effectively, influence stakeholders and drive meaningful uplift across capability, performance and culture.
  
Importantly, we are seeking someone genuinely energised by emerging technology and AI — not just someone talking about it. We want a leader who is actively curious, stays close to market developments and can identify practical ways AI and automation can improve organisational performance, efficiency and decision-making.
  

The Opportunity
Reporting into a senior technology executive, this role will:

Lead and uplift the organisation’s enterprise data and analytics capability
Help shape and drive the organisation’s evolving AI strategy and roadmap
Partner closely across cyber, technology and business teams
Improve capability, accountability and performance across the broader function
Support strong governance, cyber maturity and data protection practices
Identify practical AI and automation opportunities across the organisation
Help create a modern, commercially pragmatic and future-focused technology culture
What We’re Looking For
The successful candidate will ideally bring:

Experience leading enterprise data, analytics or information management teams
Previous hands-on experience within a cyber security role or function
Strong understanding of enterprise data environments, governance and reporting capability
Exposure to AI, automation or emerging technology initiatives within complex organisations
The ability to translate technical capability into practical business outcomes
Experience leading organisational change and uplifting underperforming or stagnant teams
Strong stakeholder engagement and leadership capability
A naturally curious mindset and passion for continuous learning and emerging technology
This role would suit a leader who has evolved from technical foundations into broader enterprise leadership and is excited by the opportunity to help shape a modern, AI-enabled and data-driven organisation.
 

Apply Now

Company Description

At NEXTDC we are building the heart of Australia & Asia’s high-tech future. We design, build and operate world class, next generation data centres, using cutting edge, environmentally efficient infrastructure to service the specific requirements of local and international customers.

NEXTDC is an exciting ASX 100-listed technology company, only 15 years young and operating 17 data centres across 7 major cities in Australia, with multiple international sites on their journey to supporting our customer’s digital infrastructure.

At NEXTDC, we know that our success depends on the talents of our people, and we foster a culture of continued learning and development. Our culture is built on collaboration and innovation, we are passionate about what we do, and we are committed to our mission to be the leading customer-centric data centre services company.

Where Cloud lives and AI thrives

 

Job Description

As our Head of Global Integrated Operations, you’ll lead and evolve NEXTDC’s 24/7 Global Integrated Operations Centre (GIOC), ensuring consistent incident management, operational oversight and communication throughout our organisation and customers.

You will play a key role in aligning operational teams, improving data visibility and driving strategic initiatives that support our growth and customer commitments.

Key responsibilities include:

Leading the 24/7 GIOC to ensure operational visibility and accountability across the business
Driving continuous service improvement and operational excellence across global teams
Overseeing incident, issue and action management to ensure consistency, tracking and resolution
Delivering operational reporting and KPI monitoring aligned to customer and internal SLAs
Supporting the implementation of strategic initiatives across NEXTDC in partnership with executive leadership
Enhancing operational transparency through integrated data systems and reporting tools
Facilitating cross functional collaboration and communication across global teams
Supporting global budget planning and operational forecasting models.

 

We are happy for this position to be based in Sydney or Melbourne

 

Qualifications

We’re seeking a strategic operational leader with strong analytical capability and the ability to drive performance across complex, multi site environments. You are comfortable operating at the executive level and leading teams responsible for mission critical infrastructure.

Your experience should see you brining:

Strong data and analytical capability with a data-driven approach to operational strategy
Proven success leading operations in fast-paced, multi-site environments
Ability to build partnerships and collaborate across business functions
Strong problem solving and risk management capability
Exceptional leadership, communication and stakeholder engagement skills
Ability to lead, inspire and develop high-performing teams

What you’ll bring 

8+ years’ experience in leadership or senior management roles
5+ years’ experience within mission critical environments
Strong team collaboration and communication skills

 

Additional Information
Seize this exciting opportunity to join one of Australia’s fastest growing companies and play a key contributing role in the future of AI and the digital economy
Join an inclusive, diverse and values-driven working culture supporting hybrid work practices
Enjoy awesome employee benefits (gifted share options, NEXTDC days, volunteer days, professional development, holistic wellbeing and mental health programs and more)
Great opportunities to progress within our company (grow as we grow)

 

Our mission is to continue building a diverse and inclusive workforce which represents the communities in which we operate.

Make NEXTDC your next move.

Leading the delivery of our key planning processes to develop the Maintenance Renewals Strategy Budget (MRSB) on its annual cycle.

What we offer you: 
 
At Aurizon, we support the development and growth of our employees. With a national portfolio of operations, we offer long-term and exciting career opportunities.

Development and growth opportunities.

Aurizon is Australia’s largest rail freight operator and a top 100 ASX company. Each year, we deliver more than 250 million tonnes of Australian commodities right across the country. Aurizon is crucial to the Australian economy, connecting miners, primary producers and industry with international and domestic markets.  

Look to Aurizon for a diverse and collaborative culture. 

At Aurizon, we are proud of our diverse, collaborative, creative and high-performance culture. We celebrate the contribution of every employee and provide opportunities for career development in a dynamic, caring, and inclusive work environment.

Company description:



ATTURRA OPERATIONS PTY LTD



Job description:



What you'll be doing


Atturra is seeking a highly skilled Senior Technical Business Analyst with extensive Azure cloud migration experience to support a Federal Government engagement within the Cloud Business Solutions team.

This role suits a candidate passionate about innovative technology and customer transformation, with the ability to communicate effectively across technical and non-technical audiences and deliver clear, confident presentations. You will be expected to lead technical working groups and support the resolution of critical customer issues.

You will evaluate end-user business requirements, translate them into detailed technical specifications, and work with delivery teams to design and implement fit-for-purpose solutions across cloud, integration, and legacy environments.

This position is Canberra-based with hybrid working arrangements. Due to the security requirements of the project, candidates must already hold an active AGSVA NV1 Security Clearance.

Responsibilities:

Examine and evaluate current systems, processes, and data flows to identify inefficiencies, risks, and opportunities for improvement
Identify, analyse, and document system requirements (functional and non-functional), including business rules, user journeys, security, reporting, integrations, and performance expectations
Engage and liaise with end users and stakeholders to elicit additional requirements and feature enhancements, clarify priorities, and confirm expected outcomes
Facilitate requirement workshops and interviews; capture decisions, assumptions, dependencies, and constraints, and maintain a clear requirements traceability approach throughout delivery
Document interfaces between new and legacy systems, including API specifications (where applicable), data mapping, transformation rules, validation logic, and error handling
Develop and maintain analysis artefacts such as process maps, current/future state documentation, user stories/use cases, acceptance criteria, and solution design inputs
Collaborate with the IT team, architects, and developers to support solution design and delivery, ensuring requirements are understood and translated into implementable technical outcomes
Support delivery planning by estimating effort (where required), identifying risks and mitigations, and contributing to implementation and cutover planning
Validate changes by planning and executing testing activities, including system testing, integration testing support, user acceptance testing coordination, defect triage, and re-testing following fixes
Train users on new or updated systems and processes by preparing user guides, quick reference materials, and training sessions, and provide post-implementation support and troubleshooting
Ensure deadlines and budget requirements are met by monitoring progress against milestones, escalating risks/issues early, and supporting governance and status reporting
Maintain accurate documentation and change records, ensuring solutions meet internal standards, compliance obligations, and audit requirements
Stay up to date with current technologies and technical developments, and proactively recommend improvements aligned to organisational needs and industry best practice.

Working Conditions:

On-site / Hybrid
Canberra
Permanent /Contract

What will make you successful in this role?

Technical Requirements:

7+ years' experience as a Senior Technical Business Analyst / Systems Analyst delivering complex ICT programs in Federal Government environments; exposure to Law Enforcement domains and high-assurance operating models is strongly preferred
Extensive experience delivering Azure migration outcomes (re-host/re-platform where applicable), including landing zone readiness, subscription and resource governance, network connectivity, identity and access management, data migration planning, and operational handover to BAU
Strong understanding of the end-to-end build and release lifecycle, including SDLC/Agile delivery, environment promotion (DEV/TEST/UAT/PROD), DevOps ways of working, CI/CD pipelines, and change/release management controls
Ability to interrogate technical solutions effectively asking the right questions to surface constraints and non-functional requirements across architecture, security, networking, data, integration, and operations; able to challenge assumptions and drive technical clarity
Proven capability to produce and maintain technical analysis/design artefacts including system context diagrams, interface/API specifications, data models and mappings, sequence/interaction flows, integration patterns, and detailed functional/non-functional specifications
Strong knowledge of enterprise business information systems, including application architecture concepts, data flows, batch/real-time integrations, identity and security considerations, logging/monitoring, and reporting/analytics dependencies
Ability to translate complex technical detail for mixed audiences (engineers, architects, cyber, operations, and business stakeholders) and to document decisions
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills, including root-cause analysis, impact assessment, dependency/risk identification, and translating findings into implementable technical requirements and acceptance criteria
MUST hold an active AGSVA NV1 Security Clearance
Effective written and verbal communication skills, with both technical and non-technical audiences.
Strong interpersonal skills and ability to work effectively with a wide range of individuals in a diverse community.

Why is this an exciting opportunity?

Atturra is one of the fastest growing ASX-listed advisory and IT solutions companies in Australia, giving you a strong foundation to fulfil your career ambitions.

We currently have 1100+ professionals working across Australia, New Zealand, Singapore and Hong Kong who are using innovation to lead the way.

This is your chance to make an impact. Come and join our talented and creative team of change makers to redefine the future through technology.

Our Cloud Business Solutions team specialises in strategy, migration, and proactive management across large enterprise and small-scale cloud deployments. We use a tailored approach to build scalable cloud solutions that modernise IT environments, enhance productivity, and ensure business continuity.

What our people say:

"We are working towards a very bright future."

"It's a company with vision and a promising future."

Why you'll love working here

Culture of possibility

At Atturra we embrace innovation within our teams, fostering an environment of possibility and growth. We believe in creating a supportive and welcoming culture, where everyone is encouraged to achieve success through collaboration, continuous learning, and smart work practices.

A strong set of company values

Our culture is reflected by our employees who demonstrate camaraderie, innovation, dedication, respect and excellence in everything they do. These traits have become integral to the way we do business and are the values we strive for.

Valuable employee benefits

Employee benefits, recognition, and wellbeing platform
Career growth via succession planning, internal promotions, and mentorship opportunities
Ongoing investment in professional development through industry and technology certifications, and study assistance
Mental health support through our Employee Assistance Program
Support for family and caring responsibilities, including paid parental leave
Employee referral program, with monetary incentives offered

Commitment to supporting Australia's veterans

Atturra is proud to be recognised as a Veteran Friendly Employer by the Department of Veterans Affairs' Veteran Employment Commitment (VEC) and is committed to supporting Australia's veteran community. We aim to enhance the employment experience for veterans by improving recruitment, support, retention, and leadership opportunities to ensure they have a meaningful and impactful career with us.

What our people say:

"Atturra has a great working environment and management are very supportive of their staff. We celebrate success and have fun while working hard."

How to apply

Careers | Atturra

An exciting opportunity has arisen for a Data Engineering Specialist to join a highly respected organisation in Melbourne, where your expertise will directly influence the ongoing development and optimisation of enterprise data solutions. This role is perfect for someone who thrives on supporting business transformation through robust data engineering practices, ensuring that data-driven decision-making is at the heart of organisational success.

What you'll do:

As a Data Engineering Specialist based in Melbourne, you will immerse yourself in a variety of engaging projects that underpin the organisation's commitment to leveraging enterprise data for business efficiency. Your day-to-day activities will involve collaborating with diverse teams to develop innovative solutions for the ongoing enhancement of the data warehouse. You will be responsible for designing reliable ETL pipelines, implementing rigorous testing protocols, optimising analytical databases, and ensuring seamless integration between various business intelligence tools.

Provide comprehensive technical and strategic advice to senior management, staff, and stakeholders regarding all aspects of the organisation's data warehouse.
Develop and maintain robust ETL processes using Microsoft SSIS to ensure efficient loading of the data warehouse.
Design, implement, and oversee automated integration tests for all data warehouse operations to guarantee reliability and accuracy.
Create, manage, and optimise analysis services databases and cubes (SSAS), supporting advanced analytics requirements.

What you bring:

To excel as a Senior Data Engineer in this organisation, your background should reflect significant hands-on experience across multiple facets of enterprise-level data management.

Advanced Azure Data Factory (ADF) skills
Demonstrate extensive experience in database design or integration with SQL Server databases that enables you to architect scalable solutions tailored to organisational needs.
Possess advanced skills in relational database management systems (RDBMS) including proficiency with SQL language for querying complex datasets efficiently.
Exhibit deep understanding of Extraction, Transformation and Loading (ETL) processes using Microsoft SSIS which allows you to build reliable pipelines for large-scale data movement.

Robert Walters Australia is acting as an Employment Agency in relation to this vacancy.

We are seeking a hands‑on Technical Business Analyst with strong experience with Magento / Adobe Commerce.

You will have strong delivery ownership to support and enhance the B2B e‑commerce platforms. This role blends technical analysis, integrations, troubleshooting, and end‑to‑end delivery management across a distributed team. Ideal for someone who enjoys solving complex problems and driving work from idea → build → test → release.

This is not a purely a strategic or hands-off role. You will be in the detail; reading Jira tickets, reviewing developer estimates, running UAT, and writing requirements that are acted on within the same sprint.

About the Role

A hybrid Tech BA + Delivery Lead role where you'll manage BAU issues, own the backlog, coordinate offshore development, and run releases end‑to‑end. You'll work closely with the Ecommerce Manager while leading the day‑to‑day delivery workflow.

Key Responsibilities:

Manage BAU tickets (accounts, content, payments, configuration)
Troubleshoot technical issues across APIs, integrations, and data flows
Prioritise daily issues and coordinate fixes with development/test teams
Gather requirements and maintain a clear, prioritised backlog
Scope, estimate, and assign work to offshore developers
Run sprints and manage delivery independently
Coordinate testing, deployment, and stakeholder communication
Lead weekly/fortnightly small releases, plus 4-5 major releases annually
Train/coach the staff on use of E- commerce system

Key Skills Required:

Strong B2B e‑commerce background
Experience with Magento / Adobe Commerce (admin, workflows, catalogue, integrations)
Understanding of payment gateways (e.g., Stripe) and reconciliation flows
Experience with Punchout and/or EDI
Solid grasp of APIs, integrations, and data flows
Ability to troubleshoot production issues across multiple systems
Experience running sprints and managing delivery end‑to‑end
ERP‑integrated e‑commerce experience
Exposure to offshore development teams
Ability to read payloads, test APIs, and validate data

Please Note: All resumes MUST be submitted in Word format.

Helping you unlock a brighter future by investing in your development and wellbeing, offering you study leave, a day for you and professional wellbeing coaching.

REnew Pacific

Palladium is a global leader in the design, development, and delivery of Positive Impact – the intentional creation of enduring social and economic value.  We work with governments, businesses, and investors to solve the world's most pressing challenges. With a team of more than 4,000 employees operating in 90-plus countries and a global network of over 35,000 experts, we help improve economies, societies, and, most importantly, people's lives.

REnew Pacific is an AU$75M program over 4.5 years. It aims to scale and expand on the critical lessons learned in the DFAT-funded Business Partnerships Platform (BPP) pilot to support DFAT and the Australian Infrastructure Financing Facility for the Pacific (AIFFP) to address climate infrastructure mitigation, adaptation and resilience among Pacific states and help Australia be the climate infrastructure partner of choice in the Pacific.

Purpose of Position 

Reporting to the Senior MEL Manager, the MEL Manager will support REnew Pacific’s Monitoring, Evaluation and Learning (MEL) work to ensure operationalisation of the program’s MEL Framework. In doing so, the MEL Manager will contribute to ensuring REnew Pacific is enabled to report on delivery performance and development impact and adapt and adjust strategy and approach in response to lessons and other performance feedback. This will include leading on project and program level data collection; evaluation and analysis; research and learning; and reporting for both internal and external audiences.

Primary Responsibilities

Support implementation of the REnew Pacific MEL Framework, working closely with the Senior MEL Manager and the REnew Pacific Team, as well as implementation partners and project partners 

Coordinate the collection and collation of quantitative and qualitative data at individual project and whole of program levels.

Implement monitoring and evaluation methodologies, applying quality principles in data collection, as well as maintain and utilise the program management information system (MIS) as it relates to MEL data. 

Identify opportunities to strengthen the use of the program MIS to translate qualitative and quantitative data into user friendly interactive dashboards for program monitoring and reporting.

Provide technical assistance to project partners including by providing guidance, coaching and other support for project partners to undertake monitoring and reporting.

Work with third party implementation partners engaged to undertake surveys and analysis for REnew Pacific. 

Coordinate with other thematic leads including the GEDSI and Communications Advisers to capture and disseminate a range of information, knowledge and lessons for a variety ofREnew Pacific stakeholders including DFAT. 

Work with other team members to support design and delivery of internal, external and regional learning events. 

Support the completion of REnew Pacific reports, including Monthly, 6-Monthly and Annual performance reports and other client reporting requirements.

Travel to Pacific Island countries as required.

Other duties as required by the Senior MEL Manager. 

Reporting Requirements

This role reports to the Senior MEL Manager. Reporting requirements may include but are not limited to:

Attendance at team meetings and other requested meetings. 

Regular one to one meetings with line manager. 

Reporting to the Team Leader during Senior MEL Manager absences.

Minimum Education and Experience Required

Qualifications:

Advanced degree in a relevant field (for example environmental science, geography, international development, or other related disciplines); and/or

Relevant qualifications in monitoring, evaluation, research and data management desirable

Skills and Experience:

Knowledge of, or experience with, the Pacific and/or international development.

Demonstrated experience designing and implementing monitoring, reporting research and learning strategies. 

Proficiency in data visualisation tools such as Power BI, with experience in designing interactive dashboards and thematic maps to monitor program indicators and track progress.

Knowledge and understanding of DFAT aid policies and strategies, and other relevant government policies.

Excellent research, analytical, and problem-solving skills.

Highly developed communication and time-management skills.

Demonstrated experience in stakeholder consultation and engagement

Demonstrated ability to work with people from diverse backgrounds and perspectives, and a professional commitment to promote a culture of gender equality and social inclusion.

An understanding of climate change and disaster risk resilience issues in the Pacific Islands context is desirable. 

Personal Attributes:

Responsive and committed team member with the ability to work confidently under pressure

Ability to manage competing priorities in a fast-paced environment

Excellent attention to detail

A professional commitment to principles of gender equality and social inclusion, safeguards and anti-corruption

It also provides a benchmark for assessing areas of potential strength as well as the identification of potential skill gaps or areas for development and improvement.  

The Capability Framework forms the basis of how we recruit, how we lead and the behaviours we exhibit, how we manage performance excellence and develop our future workforce.

Sustainable business – going beyond compliance to ensure sustainable and ethical considerations are woven throughout everything we do. This aligns equity, diversity and inclusion; safeguarding; and environmental objectives.

Bankable Planning & Value Delivery

Drive value tracking and benefit realisation across the delivery lifecycle

Advanced Analytics & AI Innovation

Partner with Data Science teams to pilot innovative analytics and AI solutions

About You

You are a highly analytical and commercially astute strategist who thrives in fast-paced, ambiguous environments. You combine strategic thinking with hands-on execution and are passionate about delivering measurable business outcomes through data and AI.

You’ll bring:

3+ years’ experience in strategy development and execution

Experience working within a management consulting environment (top-tier consulting experience highly regarded)

Strong exposure to data, analytics and technology ecosystems

Understanding of data platforms, analytics tooling and AI experimentation

Experience translating business problems into technical and data-driven solutions

Exceptional stakeholder engagement and influencing skills across complex organisations

Preferred Experience

Category: Corporate Finance & Investment Banking (Banking & Financial Services)
Ad Text:
The Senior Analyst will own end-to-end reporting deliverables across one or more asset classes or jurisdictions and contribute to a growing programme of regulatory change.

Own daily, lifecycle and end-of-day trade reporting across ASIC, CFTC and CSA, including dispute resolution, query management and month-end reporting.
Manage exceptions, reconciliations and NACK resolution across internal systems, Broadridge insideTrack and DTCC GTR; monitor KPIs, SLAs and action logs.

Support the quarterly Quality Assurance cycle, perform root cause analysis on findings, and track remediation through Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC) software solution.

Interpret regulatory rule maps, technical specifications and guidance, and translate them into operational and system requirements.

Contribute to regulatory change programmes and engage with vendors on implementation.

Develop and maintain controls, BAU procedures and decision-tree documentation to ensure auditability and ongoing compliance.

Build strong relationships with Front Office, Legal, Compliance, Technology, counterparties.

Working knowledge of OTC derivatives products - ideally cleared interest rate derivatives and equity swaps.

Strong understanding of ASIC Derivative Transaction Rules (Reporting) 2024; experience with CFTC Parts 43/45 and CSA Trade Reporting Rules desired

Experience with reporting platforms and trade repositories - Broadridge (insideTrack, futureLandscape, totalOrder), DTCC GTR, MarkitWire and equivalents.

Advanced Excel: SQL and Python (or similar scripting) strongly preferred given the team's focus on automation and reusable tooling.

Ability to read and interpret primary regulatory sources - technical specifications, validation rules and rulemaps - and challenge interpretations against primary evidence.


Superior verbal and written communication, including the ability to write clear technical analysis for legal, compliance and front office audiences.

Critical thinking and willingness to challenge - interrogate vendor guidance and prior conclusions against primary sources.

Builder mindset - interest in developing reusable tooling and frameworks, not just solving point-in-time queries.

Self-starter, highly organised, able to work independently and take ownership.

Attention to detail, ability to meet deadlines and multitask in a fast-moving regulatory environment, and the ability to influence across all levels.


Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples are encouraged to apply.

To apply please click apply or call Chloe Maher on +61 • •••• •190 for a confidential discussion.

We are somewhat different

 

Hannover Re employs more than 4,000 people across locations worldwide. Our strength? Connecting Power: We rely on each other to achieve more.

 

Our purpose? Beyond risk sharing – we team up to create opportunity. Delivering innovative, customised solutions in an environment where yesterday’s answers may not meet tomorrow’s challenges. This mindset has made us one of the world’s leading and most profitable reinsurance groups and a trusted, reliable employer.

 

In Australia and New Zealand, Hannover Re is a major player in the Life & Health reinsurance market. Locally, we combine the agility of a small, nimble team with the financial strength and global reach of one of the world’s largest reinsurers.

 

We currently have an exciting opportunity for a Valuation and Reporting Actuary to join our Life & Health Subsidiary in Sydney.

 

About the role:

 

Reporting to the Deputy Chief Actuary, this fixed‑term (12 month) role sits within our Life & Health Actuarial team and plays a key part in delivering high‑quality valuation and financial reporting outcomes.

 

As a Valuation and Reporting Actuary, you’ll be hands‑on with quarterly valuations, experience investigations and the analysis and reporting of results under AASB 17 / IFRS 17, working closely with local and global stakeholders across Hannover Re. This is an opportunity to apply your technical expertise while contributing to process improvement and broader actuarial initiatives.

 

You can look forward to:

Playing a central role in quarterly actuarial valuations, from data validation through to analysis, calculation and review of actuarial reserves

Investigating experience results, identifying emerging trends and translating insights into meaningful business commentary

Compiling, analysing and reporting valuation outcomes across local and Group reporting bases, including AASB 17 / IFRS 17

Presenting valuation and experience results to local management and Home Office, and confidently responding to stakeholder queries

Supporting statutory reporting requirements, including actuarial inputs to APRA and Group submissions

Monitoring and reporting client‑specific experience, issues and insights on both a regular and ad‑hoc basis

Identifying and contributing to process, data and reporting improvements alongside actuarial, finance and operations colleagues

Supporting the implementation of changes in financial reporting standards and actuarial developments

Working collaboratively with teams across Actuarial, Finance, Operations, Client Services, IT/Data, Claims, Underwriting and Pricing

Contributing to a strong risk, compliance and values‑driven culture

 

You come equipped with:

 FIAA qualification (or equivalent) and five years’ (or more) experience within the life insurance industry

Proven experience in actuarial valuation and IFRS 17 reporting in Australian life insurance

Understanding of Australian life insurance legislation, prudential standards and taxation requirements

Strong analytical and problem‑solving capability

Advanced Excel skills, with experience in tools such as Power BI, SQL and R considered an advantage

Solvency II knowledge and experience desired but not required

The confidence to work independently while collaborating effectively as part of a high‑performing team

Clear written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to explain complex actuarial concepts to diverse stakeholders

A proactive mindset, curiosity for improvement and a genuine desire to continuously refine processes and outcomes

Applicants must have unrestricted rights to work in Australia (Australian citizenship or permanent residency).

 

What do we offer you:

Are our values, Responsibility, Drive and We‑Spirit aligned with yours?

 

Culture & environment

You’ll join an international organisation with short decision paths, an open feedback culture, and a strong sense of community built on respect, collaboration and mutual support.

 

Benefits

We value our people and their contribution to our shared success. Our benefits include structured onboarding, hybrid working arrangements, an Employee Assistance Program (EAP) and modern offerings focused on professional development, wellbeing and engagement.

 

Career development

You’ll bring your specialist expertise and in return we offer continuous learning, exposure to global perspectives, and the opportunity to contribute ideas, innovate and shape your career.

 

Only together can we achieve our objectives.

 

To learn more about Hannover Re Australia, visit our careers site: https://www.hannover-re.com/de/local-offices/australia/career/

 

We don’t just offer a job and a title. If you’re looking for an organisation that genuinely values its people and is Somewhat Different, we would love to hear from you.

 

Apply now!

ABOUT US

Melbourne Institute of Technology (MIT) is a leading Private Higher Education Provider with campuses in Melbourne and Sydney.  We offer undergraduate, masters by coursework and masters by research degrees in IT, Networking, Data Analytics, Business, Accounting and marketing, to International and Australian students. The institution has a large and vibrant research degree program, a variety of research events including research day, MIT and international conferences. Staff research and publications include grant funded and collaborative research programs across a variety of disciplines.

ABOUT THE ROLE 

The Research Director will provide leadership in the strategic evolution of the research culture at the Institute which includes the provisioning of appropriate infrastructure, integration of both AI research considerations and ensuring Academic Integrity in the context of AI, advising on academic and administrative resources and building effective academic and industry networks to meet quality standards in accordance with the Higher Education Standards Framework. The appointee will direct the academic research and research training activities and committees at the Institute and demonstrate outstanding performance and pre-eminence as a scholar of international standing as measured by indictors of activity, engagement, quality and impact in accordance with the Institute’s academic performance framework. Previous Level E roles (preferably in Australia) or a minimum of five years at Level D

demonstrated continuing involvement in research training and leadership of learning

demonstrated sustained engagement and achievement in senior academic administrative leadership

Strong record of research supervision outcomes with evidence of innovation and effectiveness

Demonstrated evidence of academic leadership and team approaches

Demonstrated evidence of mentoring and management of academic research staff

Record of successful competitive research grant applications

Job Title: Business Analysis Lead - Centre of Excellence
Ad Text:
Take hold of a monster opportunity and join our Transformation team to drive our Business Analysis capability and Centre of Excellence function.  If you’re a good fit for this role and live in Brisbane, Sydney or Melbourne, we’d love you to apply.

Why join us?

You'll get 14% super as standard, to help your future come alive.
Unleash your potential and build a career to be proud of through excellent training and development opportunities.
Balance your work between in-person days at our purpose-built hubs and working from home.
In the office, enjoy end of trip facilities, a yoga and multi-faith room, parent and carer rooms, quiet rooms, BrewHub coffee facilities, and collaborative team spaces.

About the role

We’re seeking an accomplished Business Analysis Leader who has progressed their career delivering complex change and is now ready to step into a role with broader impact - shaping the quality, capability and consistency of Business Analysis across an organisation.

This is a rare opportunity to move beyond hands‑on project delivery and shape how Business Analysis is practised across a large, evolving organisation. Rather than being embedded in a single initiative, you’ll operate as a trusted advisor and capability leader—working alongside Business Analysts, delivery teams and senior stakeholders to lift the quality, consistency and impact of Business Analysis across major transformation initiatives.

You’ll bring the credibility of real delivery experience and use it to coach, guide and uplift others, ensuring Business Analysis consistently drives better outcomes at scale. This role is a permanent opportunity. 

 

Day to day, you'll:

Partnering with Business Analysts and delivery teams to enhance the quality, consistency and impact of Business Analysis
Designing and evolving practical, fit-for-purpose frameworks, tools and standards that are actively used in delivery
Acting as a coach and trusted advisor, helping BAs navigate complexity, stakeholder dynamics and ambiguity
Building and maintaining a centralised toolkit of templates, guidance and proven approaches
Driving capability uplift through training, mentoring and hands-on coaching
Influencing senior stakeholders to ensure robust analysis informs decision-making and delivery outcomes
Monitoring adoption and effectiveness of BA practices, using insights to continuously refine and improve
Championing modern, pragmatic ways of working across the BA discipline 

Why this role is different:

A specialist advisory and capability uplift role
Focused on enabling others to perform at their best
A standalone position with no direct reports, but broad influence
An opportunity for enterprise-wide impact across transformation initiatives

This role is not:

A traditional BA role embedded in a single project
A purely governance or compliance-driven function
A theoretical “best practice” role disconnected from delivery
A position focused on day-to-day detailed BA execution

About you

It goes without saying you'll be a great communicator with top notch interpersonal skills. We'll also expect you to pick up problems and come up with quick, creative ways to solve them. It's quite likely you tick some of the following boxes too:  

Significant experience as a Senior Business Analyst, Lead BA, or BA Practice Lead within a Financial Services environment
A proven track record delivering business analysis within complex transformation programs
Experience in Superannuation, Wealth Management or broader Financial Services or similarly complex environments
Strong expertise across Business Analysis frameworks, tools and techniques

But more importantly, your approach is:

Pragmatic – you know what works in real-world delivery environments
Influential – you can guide and inspire without formal authority
Coaching-led – you’re motivated by developing others and growing capability
Credible – your experience earns trust; you’ve “been there and done it”
Outcome-focused – you care about impact, not just artefacts
You’re energised by lifting the capability of others and creating lasting impact—rather than owning delivery yourself

About us

Our 2.4 million members trust us to take care of over $330 billion in retirement savings. And we help them take charge of their finances and face the future with confidence. Our inclusive culture means you’ll be valued and heard in a respectful workplace. We've created an environment where you can be yourself and do amazing work. It's that simple.

Apply online now

Everyone is welcome to apply. We value diverse thinking, cultures, perspectives, backgrounds, and abilities. Awaken your career at Australian Retirement Trust and apply now. Applications close Wednesday, 27 May 2026.

We don’t want to leave you with a monstrous wait. So, you’ll hear from us after the closing date to find out whether you’ve made it through to the next round or not. Being fair and up-front is important to us – and recruitment is no different.

Play a key role in shaping how we deliver a seamless experience for our members through insights that drive action.

About US
UniSuper is a place where brilliant thinkers and passionate people come together. As one of Australia's largest super funds, the work you'll do with us makes a meaningful difference today and tomorrow for our members, the planet and for you.

Our Service & Experience division is recognised for performance excellence and for shaping the future of retirement for our members every day. As we continue to grow, so do the opportunities for you to challenge yourself, develop new skills, and make a real impact. Within this role your ideas are valued, your ambition is celebrated and your work genuinely matters.


The Opportunity
If you are the kind of leader who can turn data into decisions that genuinely change outcomes, this is a rare opportunity. As our Manager, Workforce Optimisation & Insights, you will sit at the centre of our Service & Experience (S&E) division, helping our frontline teams deliver an easier, more seamless experience for members through sharp forecasting, smart capacity planning, and insights that lead to action. You will build on a workforce planning function that is performing strongly and has momentum, then take it further.

The opportunity here is to elevate how we use insights across the business, lift capability, and help leaders see the story behind the numbers so they can act with confidence. In this role you will have direct exposure to senior leaders and a genuine opportunity to influence decisions at pace.

You will work closely with the Data Office, partner across the division, and lead a team of specialists, supporting their ongoing development, capability growth, and impact across the business.

In this role, you will:


Lead short, medium and long-term workforce forecasting, capacity and resource planning across customer channels.
Build and embed workforce management strategies that improve agility, productivity, and cost-to-serve outcomes.
Translate complex data into clear insights, recommendations, and initiatives that leaders can act on.
Lift the maturity of the insights function by shaping direction, improving storytelling, and linking insights to operational actions.
Partner with the Data Office through established cadences to improve alignment, ways of working, and outcomes from shared data priorities.
Lead and develop a team of workforce planning and insights specialists, building bench strength, cross-skilling, and a high engagement culture.
About you
You are an exceptional operator and leader who can move comfortably between strategy and detail. You know how to bring people with you, lift capability, and create clarity in complex environments. You are curious, action oriented, and have a strong instinct for what will genuinely improve service outcomes and operational performance.

Most importantly, you know how to turn insight into action. You can take a room of leaders from "here are the numbers" to what it means, the decision we need to make, and what we do next. You build trust through practical thinking, strong communication, and a clear focus on outcomes.

You will also bring:


Proven people leadership experience, with a track record of coaching, developing, and uplifting capability in specialist teams.
Strong data and insights background, including translating analysis into recommendations and actions at leadership level.
Experience in workforce planning and workforce management, including forecasting, capacity planning, and resource modelling.
Confidence with workforce management platforms and tools, ideally including Genesys Cloud CX and or workforce management software.
Strong capability in business intelligence, dashboards and reporting, with the ability to simplify and communicate a clear narrative for different audiences.
Pragmatic, outcomes-focused style with strong stakeholder management, influencing and negotiation skills.
What US offer
We believe that the best way to achieve great things is when we come together and collaborate. Therefore, we ask you to be able to commit to 60% of your time in office.

UniSuper's high performance culture is supported by a range of benefits that demonstrates our commitment to ensuring our employees achieve their full potential, thrive, and grow their career with us. These include:


A personal development budget of $1500 to support employees in their professional growth
Community, Culture & Wellbeing leave
Mental physical and financial well-being initiatives
At UniSuper, we value and celebrate diversity and inclusion in our workplace, where everybody can thrive. We believe that having a variety of perspectives, backgrounds, interests, abilities, and skills is crucial for delivering great retirement outcomes for our members. We invite you to apply for the roles that suit your career aspirations, even if you don't meet all the requirements listed.

Think Great. Create Great.

Lead Data Scientist

We are hiring a Lead Data Scientist to own and build the data science function from the ground up. This is a hands-on, high-impact role focused on turning complex product, customer and operational data into measurable business value.
This is not a strategy-only position. You will design the approach, build the models, engineer pipelines, create insights, and establish the data practices that support a growing AI-powered platform. You will work closely with engineering, product, customer success, operations, finance and leadership to define what data means across the business and how it can drive better decisions.
About the role As the first dedicated data science hire, you will have the opportunity to shape the company’s data strategy, build the foundations of a modern data stack, and create the models, dashboards and reporting frameworks that teams rely on.
You will work across machine learning, applied AI, data engineering, analytics, governance and business intelligence. The role is suited to someone who is highly technical, commercially minded and comfortable operating in a fast-moving environment where they can build from scratch.

Key responsibilities
Design, build and deploy machine learning models and statistical analyses that solve real business problems
Work hands-on with LLM and RAG system data, including retrieval quality, conversation outcomes, latency and model performance
Build experimentation frameworks, including A/B testing and causal inference, to validate product and business decisions
Develop NLP and text analytics capability to extract insights from conversation data at scale
Architect scalable data pipelines across cloud platforms such as AWS, GCP or Azure
Build and maintain a modern data stack across ingestion, transformation, warehousing, orchestration and data quality
Establish data governance practices including cataloguing, lineage, access controls and compliance frameworks
Create dashboards, scorecards and executive reporting across product, engineering, customer success, finance, operations and leadership
Define key metrics across customer experience, product reliability, unit economics and operational health
Translate ambiguous business questions into clear, measurable data problems
Build the business case for growing the data function and hire or mentor future team members as needed
What you will bring
8–10+ years’ experience across data science, data engineering, analytics or applied AI/ML
Proven experience building and deploying machine learning models in production
Strong Python and SQL capability
Experience with modern data tooling such as dbt, Airflow, Dagster, Spark, Snowflake, BigQuery or Redshift
Experience architecting cloud-native data platforms across AWS, GCP or Azure
Hands-on experience with LLM-based systems, RAG architectures, vector databases and orchestration frameworks
Strong statistical foundations across experimental design, causal inference, regression, classification and time-series analysis
Experience with data governance, privacy and compliance in enterprise or regulated environments
Ability to communicate complex data insights to senior stakeholders and non-technical audiences
Experience in SaaS, AI/ML product companies or enterprise implementation environments
Nice to have
Experience working in the Australian market and familiarity with Australian privacy legislation
Familiarity with conversational AI metrics such as deflection, containment, intent accuracy and ASR/TTS performance
MLOps experience across model versioning, monitoring and CI/CD for ML pipelines
A quantitative degree in computer science, statistics, mathematics, physics, engineering or similar
Cloud or data certifications such as AWS Solutions Architect or GCP Professional Data Engineer
What success looks like In the first 30 days, you will complete a deep immersion into the product, data landscape and AI architecture, assess the current state of data, and deliver a clear roadmap covering risks, quick wins, metrics and priorities.
Over the following months, you will strengthen core data platform components, launch operational scorecards, improve data quality and model performance, and establish reporting rhythms across the business.
Over 6–12 months, you will have built a trusted data foundation, improved business outcomes through data science, matured metric ownership, and developed a clear plan for growing the data function.
Benefits
Join a fast-growing, innovative technology environment
Build and lead a data function from the ground up
Work at the forefront of AI, data science and conversational technology
High level of ownership, autonomy and business impact
Strong career growth and professional development opportunities
Competitive salary
Flexible, remote-first working environment

Job Title: Quality and Service Assurance Transformation Lead
Ad Text:
Lead the end-to-end service assurance and quality framework
Transform QA using automation, AI insights and system-led controls
Attractive hybrid work model | Energy Discounts |Career Growth Opportunities

Alinta Energy is one of Australia’s biggest energy retailers, generators and developers with over 1.1 million customers. Australia’s energy system is undergoing the biggest transformation in its history and we’re right there with it. We’re investing in and developing large-scale renewables and storage projects as part of our ongoing effort to provide our customers with reliable, affordable and sustainable energy.

We understand that flexibility matters when considering your next role. Let us know your requirements, and we’ll carefully consider how we might accommodate them.

Ready to make a real impact?

As the Service Assurance Functional Owner, you will step into a newly created, highly strategic role responsible for defining and governing Alinta Energy’s end-to-end service assurance model across Customer Operations. The focus of this role is quality and assurance transformation - shifting the organisation from largely manual, retrospective Quality Assurance to proactive, system-enabled assurance driven by data, automation and AI insights.

You will own quality standards, assurance frameworks and knowledge governance, ensuring regulatory, customer and commercial outcomes are consistently delivered at scale. Working closely with Operations, Product, Digital and Technology teams, you will embed assurance logic directly into platforms such as Genesys, enabling automated monitoring, real-time guidance and meaningful reporting.

This role provides clear visibility of how people, processes and systems are performing, translating quality data, complaints and risk indicators into actionable improvement opportunities. Presenting regularly to senior leaders, you will influence change across the business, while accountability for delivery remains with functional owners.

Why you’ll love working here

At Alinta Energy, we’re taking real action to deliver reliable and sustainable energy for Australia - and that practical approach runs through everything we do. You’ll get clear direction, genuine flexibility, and the support you need to grow, all while working on projects that make a lasting difference. We’re big enough to create impact, but small enough that your ideas and effort will always be seen.

There are plenty of other great benefits of working for Alinta Energy, including flexible working, energy discounts, an Employee Assistance Program (EAP), a paid volunteer day each year, fully stocked kitchens, and lots more.

What you’ll do

Own and govern the end-to-end service assurance and quality framework
Define quality standards aligned to regulatory, CX and commercial outcomes
Design system-led and AI-assisted assurance models to reduce manual QA
Lead analysis of quality, complaints and operational risk drivers
Translate insight into cross-functional improvement initiatives
Own knowledge governance, standards and platform integration
Partner with Product and Technology to embed assurance into workflows

Your experience

Experience in service assurance, quality or regulatory environments supporting large-scale service or digital transformation initiatives
Strong background in quality frameworks and control design
Experience with telephony platforms and automated QA or AI insights
Ability to analyse complex workflows and identify systemic risk
Proven stakeholder influence across operations, technology and leaders
Background in highly regulated industries (energy, banking, insurance or similar)
Experience with Genesys is preferred, but not required

Please note this role requires full working rights within Australia.

This role suits a strategic, commercially minded leader who thrives in complex environments and enjoys driving large-scale transformation through data, technology and strong stakeholder influence.

Like what you’re seeing?

If you like the sound of this, and you’ve got experience in a similar role, we’d love to talk to you. Come and discover better together with Alinta Energy.

We are a 2026 Circle Back Initiative Employer - we commit to respond to every applicant.

If your application progresses, we will need to complete background and pre-employment checks, such as immigration checks, reference checks, criminal background checks, qualification checks, and additional checks for roles related to finance.

Our Diversity Commitment

Alinta Energy offers a truly diverse, safe and supportive work environment. As an equal opportunity employer, Alinta Energy encourages applications from any qualified person, including First Nations Australians, members of the LGBTQIA+ community, people of all ages and people living with a disability. We are also proud to be a WORK180 Endorsed Employer and we are committed to advancing women’s careers.

We look forward to hearing from you.

Click ‘Apply’ to submit your application.

At Wesfarmers Health we strive to make health, beauty and wellness experiences simpler, more affordable and easier to access for all Australians.

Our portfolio includes well-known names like Priceline, as well as our MediAesthetics brands - Clear Skincare Clinics and SILK Laser Clinics. In the digital space, we're proud to have SISU Health and InstantScripts—and most recently, we've launched our newest retail beauty stores, atomica.

What began in 1910 as a small co-operative of just three pharmacies has grown into Wesfarmers Health—a leading Australian organisation in health, beauty, and wellness.

Today, Wesfarmers Health proudly employs over 3,600 team members, all committed to delivering accessible, innovative, and trusted health and beauty services to Australians nationwide, across retail, medi-aesthetics, digital health, and wholesale and supply chain operations.

Reporting to the FP&A Manager, you'll partner with senior stakeholders across the business and play a key role in driving performance insights, corporate planning, and continuous improvement.

What you'll be doing:

Leading end-to-end monthly forecasting processes across the business
Supporting month-end close, including variance analysis, insights, and performance commentary
Delivering weekly trading and board-level reporting, including actionable insights for senior leadership
Playing a key role in the annual budget and Five-Year Strategic Plan processes
Preparing quarterly business review materials and supporting executive and investor reporting requirements
Conducting scenario modelling and ad-hoc analysis to support strategic projects and decision-making
Monitoring working capital and cash flow forecasting to enhance financial visibility
Building strong, trusted relationships with senior stakeholders across Finance, Strategy, and operations
Driving continuous improvement through enhanced reporting, automation, and process optimisation

What you'll bring:

Demonstrated experience in a fast paced and high performing environment
Proficiency in building financial models, conducting scenario analysis and financial analysis
Proven ability to develop effective partnerships to proactively find solutions and efficiencies in processes
Ability to work effectively prioritise and develop workplans to achieve deadlines
Experience using large ERP systems & BI tools preferred. SAP, BPC and OneStream for financial reporting and analysis
Prior experience within FMCG, Retail or Pharmaceutical industries and/or at an ASX listed company is an advantage
Critical and objective thinker with strong, cross functional problem-solving skills
Tertiary qualifications in Accounting or Finance related discipline
CPA/CA qualification

Why us?

Access to employee benefits across Wesfarmers including team member discounts at Bunnings, Kmart, Target, Officeworks, OnePass, Priceline, atomica and Clear Skincare Clinics
Ongoing professional development and career opportunities across the Wesfarmers Health Division and the broader Wesfarmers Group
Novated leasing options
Access to our Employee Assistance Program (EAP) - available to all team members and their immediate family members, 24/7, 365 days a year
Beautiful outdoor terrace for work and recreation
Food, coffee and health & wellbeing facilities onsite including a landlord operated gym
Dedicated end of trip facilities (cycle racks, showers, lockers)

Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Applicants

Wesfarmers Health aims to become an employer of choice for First Nations Australians, through investments that attract, empower and retain First Nations team members within our stores, distribution centres, clinics and corporate offices.

As part of the Wesfarmers group, we provide authentic support through a diverse range of programs and initiatives, designed to empower you on your journey towards personal achievement and professional advancement.

A Federal Government department is seeking a Lead Data Analyst to assist in the design and development of high-quality visualisations and solutions in support of business and technical requirements and for solving highly complex problems.

This role will be focused around building fit-for-purpose analytics solutions within a large EDW environment. The work will involve connecting source system data to front end reporting products (Power BI, SAP BO etc).

You will be responsible for performing detailed analysis and evaluation of complex business processes and requirements. Managing the design, development and maintenance of enterprise data warehouse/data lake. Additionally managing the development of data engineering frameworks and best practices for processes aimed at enhancing the availability, security and performance of data assets.

Contract Details:

Initial 12-month contract with 2 x 12-month extension options

Estimated start date July 2026 (pending onboarding and Availability)

Baseline Security Clearance required (you must be an Australian Citizen to go through the vetting process).

Full time 40-hour work week

This role can be located in ACT or VIC.

Hybrid working arrangement. Mix of onsite and WFH - The contractor is expected to work onsite for the first 6 weeks before a hybrid flexible working arrangement can be considered.




Key responsibilities:

Responsible for the design and development of high-quality solutions in support of business and technical requirements and for solving highly complex problems.

Perform detailed analysis and evaluation of complex business processes and requirements

Manage the design, development and maintenance of enterprise data warehouse/data lake

Solve complex problems and deliver solutions to perform high-level analysis of technical requirements to support project evaluations

Apply a variety of end-to-end visualisation techniques and designs the content/appearance of data visuals.

 

Desired experience:

Experience working with reporting/data visualisation products (Power BI, Tableau or Qlik).

Experience in developing Reports in SAP Business Objects (Desirable)

An understanding of or experience with large Teradata EDW environments would be desirable

What are we looking for?

The Senior Evaluator will have a primary focus on Program, Process, and Impact Evaluation, as well as on implementing the Treaty Authority’s Monitoring, Evaluation, and Learning (MEL) Framework. The role will lead the design and delivery of evaluation processes, program logic models, and contribute to performance indicators and reporting protocols that enhance accountability and continuous quality improvement across the organisation’s work.

Working collaboratively across teams, the Evaluator / Senior Evaluator will strengthen the organisation's capacity to measure impact, embed learning, and communicate outcomes credibly internally and externally. This role requires both technical and practical expertise in program, process, and impact evaluation, as well as MEL implementation, and a strong understanding of First Peoples’ cultures, histories, and rights.

It represents a significant opportunity to contribute directly to treaty-making, social justice, and reconciliation in Victoria.

What you need to succeed:

We are seeking a Senior Evaluator to lead the design and delivery of program evaluation across the Treaty Authority. Key responsibilities include:

Developing and refining program logic models, performance indicators, and evaluation plans to measure outcomes and impact.
Providing technical advice and capacity building to support teams in implementing culturally strong evaluation methods.
Ensuring evaluation findings inform decision-making, accountability, and continuous quality improvement.
Overseeing the implementation of the Treaty Authority’s MEL Framework, embedding learning and reflection across the organisation.
Supporting teams to integrate monitoring processes into their work, ensuring consistency and quality in data collection and reporting.
Facilitating organisational learning cycles by analysing and sharing evaluation insights with Members, staff, and stakeholders.
Building trusted relationships with partners, communities, and knowledge holders to ensure MEL processes are participatory and culturally grounded.
Applying and strengthening the Treaty Authority’s frameworks, policies, and practices that uphold Indigenous Data Sovereignty (IDSov) and Indigenous Data Governance (IDGov).

About the Treaty Authority

The Treaty Authority is an independent entity with a mandate to facilitate the formation of treaties between the State of Victoria and First Peoples in Victoria.

The Treaty Authority’s work is grounded in Aboriginal culture, lore and law and led collectively by a panel of five Members committed to Aboriginal self-determination and empowerment. It engages with a broad cross-section of stakeholders including the State of Victoria, the First Peoples’ Assembly and Traditional Owners in Victoria.

The objects of the Treaty Authority’s work are to:

Facilitate the journey towards reconciliation, mutual respect and tolerance between First Peoples and other people of the State and to
Maintain, promote and advance the self-determination and empowerment, culture and human rights of First Peoples.

What you’ll bring:

We are seeking a highly skilled individual with:

Lived experience, knowledge and understanding of Victorian First Peoples communities.
Relevant tertiary education qualifications, ideally with practical experience in program, process, and impact evaluation, along with Monitoring, Evaluating & Learning (MEL) implementation.
Demonstrated experience designing and delivering evaluations using a variety of frameworks and methodologies, especially in First Peoples’ contexts.
Experience supporting teams to embed monitoring, reflection and quality improvement processes.
Proven ability to engage and communicate sensitively and effectively with First Peoples, applying proper negotiation and consultation practices.
Knowledge of cultural safety, intercultural communication, and Indigenous evaluative paradigms.
Excellent time and project management skills.
Commitment to reforms that benefit First Peoples in Victoria.
Strategic and critical thinker that can link organisational objectives to evaluation and research practices.
