Rhelm is a specialist consulting company and 2026 Client Choice Award Winner offering Advisory, Environment & Planning, Community & Place, Infrastructure and Natural Hazards & Resilience services to clients in Australia and internationally. We are passionate about the environment, sustainable development, quality technical advice and planning for the future through informed decision-making.  More details are available at www.rhelm.com.au

About the role 

We are currently recruiting for multiple roles from mid-career to senior in our Advisory team discipline. The role will apply economic theory and practice in the context of state, national and international standards to support infrastructure and policy development. You will have the opportunity to be involved in projects across the transport, property, water, environment, education and health disciplines. You will undertake qualitative and quantitative analysis and independent research, including economic appraisal, impact assessment, financial modelling, and policy analysis. Your work will contribute directly to business cases, strategic advisory, and evaluation frameworks that inform major investment decisions.

You will manage or support projects from inception through to delivery, working closely with senior staff to deliver high-quality outputs within time and budget. Collaboration is central to the role, with regular engagement across multidisciplinary teams and with clients including government agencies, infrastructure providers and development partners.

At Rhelm, we value analytical rigour and clear communication. You will be expected to translate complex analysis into practical insights that support informed decision-making.

Skills

The successful candidate will:

Have an undergraduate degree in economics, commerce or finance or equivalent and at least 5 years work experience

Already have full Australian working rights

Proficient in cost benefit analysis and development of spreadsheet models

Have a keen interest and/or relevant experience in how economics can inform decision making and policy development

Have lateral thinking skills and be highly motivated

Be willing to learn and apply what you have learned already

Have ability to work independently and collaborate meaningfully in a team environment.

The following would be an advantage:

Experience with QGIS or ArcGIS and Python

Dual degree in related field (eg. engineeering, science, planning)

Experience in managing large datasets

Experience in project management

Strong written and verbal communication skills

Benefits and perks

Work alongside technical leaders in the industry.

Opportunity to work on a diverse range of projects.

Great mentoring and support in the development of your career.

A dynamic company with an opportunity to grow your career

Job Title: Senior Business Analyst - Lending & CRM Platforms
Ad Text:
An exciting opportunity exists for an experienced Senior Business Analyst to join a purpose-led financial services organisation delivering key lending, CRM and platform transformation initiatives.

This role will play a critical part in driving the enhancement and optimisation of core business platforms, working closely with business stakeholders, delivery teams and technology partners to deliver scalable and effective solutions aligned to strategic objectives.

Key Responsibilities:

Engage with business stakeholders to gather, analyse and document functional requirements
Translate business needs into clear business specifications, user stories and acceptance criteria
Support platform enhancement and transformation initiatives across lending and CRM environments
Conduct process analysis to identify opportunities for improvement, automation and optimisation
Collaborate with developers, architects and delivery teams to support solution design and implementation
Support testing activities including test scenarios, UAT coordination and validation of business outcomes
Facilitate workshops and stakeholder engagement sessions across business and technology teams
Maintain documentation and supporting artefacts within Agile delivery environments

About You:

You will be a strong end-to-end Business Analyst with experience operating within banking or financial services environments.

You will ideally bring:

Experience within business, corporate or institutional banking environments
Exposure to lending platforms, loan origination or credit approval workflows
Experience working across CRM or Salesforce-enabled environments
Strong Agile delivery experience, including user stories and backlog management
Excellent stakeholder engagement and workshop facilitation capability
Strong process mapping, documentation and business analysis skills
Experience working closely with technical delivery teams, developers and solution architects
Familiarity with tools such as Jira, Confluence and Azure DevOps (ADO)

Highly regarded:

Salesforce platform exposure
Lending transformation programme experience

This is an opportunity to work on meaningful, enterprise-scale transformation initiatives within a complex and evolving financial services environment. The role offers a strong collaborative culture, a hybrid working environment and is based in Sydney CBD.

Please note that Australian Citizenship is essential and candidates must be eligible to obtain and maintain a Baseline Security Clearance. If this opportunity aligns with your background and experience, please apply now or reach out for a confidential discussion.

Contact Nick Largent for more information.

About Us

UniSuper is a workplace where highly skilled professionals are empowered to Think Great and deliver exceptional retirement outcomes for our members. With a culture grounded in accountability, collaboration and care, your work contributes directly to members' long term financial security.
Step into a high impact role where you help shape how UniSuper works, supporting teams and leaders to embed agile ways of working during a critical period of transformation.

The role
The key focus of this role is to deliver hands on coaching, training and facilitation that accelerates adoption of UniSuper's new operating model and agile ways of working. You will contribute across the organisation, supporting teams and leaders to translate frameworks and principles into practical, day to day behaviours that improve delivery outcomes.

* Deliver immersive coaching to teams and leaders transitioning to the new operating model
* Apply agile and lean practices to improve ways of working at team, squad and portfolio levels
* Support leaders to understand and apply their evolving roles within agile delivery
* Design and deliver engaging training, workshops and retrospectives for diverse audiences
* Analyse adoption health and identify opportunities for continuous improvement
* Contribute to operating model documentation, playbooks and ways of working guides

About you
You are an experienced agile or delivery coach who enjoys influencing change through collaboration, insight and practical support. You bring strong judgement, adaptability and a passion for building capability in complex environments.

* Significant experience as an agile coach, delivery coach or change practitioner
* Proven ability to design and deliver agile training for varied stakeholder groups
* Deep knowledge of agile and lean frameworks, applied pragmatically to context
* Experience supporting large scale operating model or organisational change
* Strong facilitation and communication skills, translating complexity into action
* Comfort working in, or alongside, regulated or enterprise environments

What We Offer
UniSuper believes that the best way to achieve great things is when we come together and collaborate. Therefore, we ask you to be able to commit to 60% of your time in office. UniSuper is proud of our culture and benefits, which empower our people to achieve their full potential, thrive, and grow their career with us. These include:

* 17% employer superannuation contributions
* Flexible working and multiple ways to flex
* Generous paid parental leave
* Annual learning and development budget

It should go without saying, but at UniSuper, we value and celebrate diversity and inclusion. We believe that a variety of perspectives, backgrounds, interests, abilities, and skills is crucial for delivering great retirement outcomes for our members. We invite you to apply for the roles that suit your career aspirations, even if you don't meet all the requirements.

What's the role?

An exciting opportunity has become available in our Consumer division for a Pricing Manager. In this role, you will be an integral part of the dynamic, highly motivated team that supports the delivery of pricing initiatives across News Corp Australia, spanning Consumer, B2B and Tech Platform Partnerships.

Responsibilities

Drive commercial growth and consumer value by defining and optimising pricing strategies for both existing products and new propositions.

Lead the development of B2B commercial rate cards and tech platform pricing to ensure sustainable revenue through direct and partner channels.

Utilise customer research, competitive intelligence, and data analytics to provide actionable pricing recommendations and business case inputs.

Model price elasticity for cover prices and subscriptions to optimise price points and evaluate the effectiveness of tactical promotional initiatives.

Proactively manage and optimise all live price books, ensuring quality control, compliance with governance, and mitigation of pricing exceptions.

Collaborate with acquisition teams to test pricing hypotheses and align specific price points to audience segments through a rigorous, inquisitive testing framework.

Who are you?

Highly analytical with an understanding of statistical modelling, forecasting and pricing best practice. Strong commercial acumen and the ability to understand complex business drivers and structures. Experience working with large complex datasets.

The ability to identify, evaluate and develop customer value management strategies and promotional tactics that optimise customer and business outcomes for subscriptions and circulation.

Strong customer and commercial leadership, presentation skill and stakeholder management to optimise consumer value for the business 

Experience in B2B or platform/partnership pricing and commercial rate cards is highly regarded

Project management skills to support business case development and stakeholder engagement through to Senior Leadership decision support. 

Advanced Excel skills 

Why News Corp Australia

Join a culture where innovation is our operating rhythm. You’ll work on a diverse portfolio of Australia’s most loved brands and play a pivotal role in leading the technological shift in how we create commercial content. Our vibrant, hybrid work environment and dynamic, values-driven culture empowers you to push boundaries, collaborate as one unified team, and make a positive, measurable impact for all Australians.

News Extras Benefits is a commitment to our people for their valued contribution to News, our audiences, clients and communities.

Our benefits include:

Exclusively ours - Access to publications and products from across the News Corp family

Perks & discounts - Exclusive offers across a range of products and services that deliver genuine cost savings and benefits

Health & wellbeing - Advice, support and facilities for healthy minds and bodies (including an onsite gym at our Adelaide, Brisbane & Sydney offices)

Time off - News offers a range of leave options to support your personal needs and moments that matter in life: Parental leave, Birthday leave, Volunteer leave, Career break and Compassionate leave.

Financial wellbeing - Support on making the most of your money

In addition to our perks and benefits, employees have access to a number of initiatives and programs that support their wellbeing and professional growth, such as:

Helping you grow - Access tailored development opportunities, including leadership programs, to cultivate your skills and support your career

Giving back - Make a difference to our communities through volunteer opportunities and company-organised charity drives

Workplace Inclusion and facilities – Engage with your passions through various employee-resource groups and accessibility to multi-faith quiet rooms & parents’ rooms

Who are we?

In an evolving media industry our brands continue to drive the story and influence the agenda - 7.4 million Australians read one of our national, metro or regional paid daily titles a week. We are proud to be the greatest storytellers in Australia. Our products are innovative and dynamic, constantly adapting to new challenges and pioneering the way forward to lead the market. A fast paced and ever changing environment requires adequate, robust and reliable business systems and process which play a vital part in our success.

What’s next?

Apply now or for more information on careers at News Corp Australia visit www.newscorpaustralia.com/careers

Our Commitment to Diversity, Equity and Inclusion

Diversity strengthens our national teams and newsroom as we seek to tell stories that represent the communities of Australia. Our people make us unique, and so we are committed to equal employment opportunities regardless of race, religion, sexual orientation, age, disability or gender identity. Applicants of diverse backgrounds are encouraged to apply!

We are committed to ensuring an accessible and supportive recruitment process. If you require any adjustments or accommodations at any stage, please do not hesitate to let us know here. In order for us to assist you, please ensure you include the role title you are applying for in the subject line. For more information regarding Diversity at NewsCorp Australia please visit www.newscorpaustralia.com/careers under the Diversity@News section.

An exciting opportunity is available within a high-performing Middle Office function at a leading financial services firm operating across global markets. This role sits within the Trade Reporting team, supporting OTC derivatives regulatory reporting across multiple jurisdictions.

This is a great opportunity for someone with derivatives operations or regulatory reporting experience who is looking to step into a more technical, ownership-driven role within a fast-paced and evolving environment.


About the Role

You will be responsible for end-to-end OTC derivatives trade reporting across ASIC, CFTC and CSA requirements, ensuring accurate, timely and compliant reporting across multiple asset classes including rates and equity derivatives.

The role offers strong exposure to regulatory change, automation initiatives, and cross-functional collaboration across front office, compliance, technology and external counterparties.


Key Responsibilities

Own daily and lifecycle trade reporting across multiple regulatory jurisdictions
Manage breaks, exceptions, reconciliations and NACK resolution across reporting platforms
Monitor KPIs, SLAs and reporting controls to ensure ongoing compliance
Support regulatory reporting QA cycles and conduct root cause analysis on issues
Maintain and enhance BAU procedures, controls and documentation
Contribute to regulatory change projects and system enhancements
Engage with internal stakeholders including front office, legal, compliance and technology teams
Liaise with external vendors, trade repositories and industry utilities

Experience in derivatives operations, middle office or regulatory reporting
Strong understanding of OTC derivatives (rates and/or equity derivatives preferred)
Exposure to trade reporting regimes such as ASIC, CFTC and/or CSA highly regarded
Experience with platforms such as DTCC GTR, Broadridge or similar
Strong Excel skills; SQL or Python exposure advantageous
High attention to detail with strong problem-solving ability
Comfortable working in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment
Strong communication skills with the ability to engage across technical and non-technical stakeholders

If you're looking for a role where you can deepen your regulatory reporting expertise and work closely with both business and technology teams, this could be an excellent next step.

Job Title: Head of Finance - TTV Reporting & FP&A
Ad Text:
Company Description
Nine is Australia's largest locally owned media company. Working at Nine, you'll have access to a unique range of experiences and opportunities, helping drive the success of the country's most trusted television, radio, digital and publishing brands.
Our content reaches almost every Australian - meaning what we do has real impact. We bring people together, celebrate the big moments, and capture the everyday ones.
Some of our most beloved brands have been part of Australian life for generations, and others - new on the scene - have already found their place firmly in our lives. We're evolving and we need people like you to bring new ideas, innovate and make your mark.


Job Description

Reporting to the Chief Financial Officer (Total Television), you will lead the reporting and FP&A functions across some of Nine's premier assets, including Nine Network and 9Now. In this pivotal 14 month parental leave contract, you will act as a key strategic partner to executive leadership, providing the financial insights and commercial foresight necessary to navigate and thrive within a rapidly evolving digital media landscape.

Day to day you will:

Lead and mentor a high-performing finance team to drive operational and commercial success.
Direct month-end reporting and statutory compliance in line with Australian Accounting Standards.
Lead financial cycles, including annual budgeting, monthly forecasting, and long-range planning.
Deliver strategic insights to the Executive team regarding performance, risks, and opportunities.
Partner with Strategy to build business cases supporting the transition to digital streaming.
Manage audit relationships and ensure the integrity of half-year and year-end reporting.
Optimise reporting processes through automation to increase speed and accuracy of insights.

What you'll bring:

CA/CPA qualification and significant prior experience in progressive finance leadership roles.
Strong leadership skills with a proven ability to mentor teams and build trust quickly.
Expertise in FP&A, including management reporting, forecasting, and balance sheet control.
Commercial acumen to translate complex financial data into actionable business strategies.
Advanced technical proficiency in Excel, financial systems, and modern forecasting tools.
Stakeholder management skills to engage effectively with senior leaders and external auditors.

Additional Information

How we work
At Nine, our flexible work options vary by role and team. Depending on the position, this may include flexible hours, hybrid work, or part-time arrangements. We welcome discussing your flexibility needs during the hiring process - just ask the Talent Acquisition team.

Our employee benefits include:

18 weeks paid parental leave with no distinction between primary and secondary carers
Access to 'Employee Exclusives' program - a way of getting closer to our incredible brands, offering unique experiences, behind-the-scenes access, and awesome perks.
Digital newspaper subscription to our mastheads
Annual gift voucher for Stan subscription
More info at Nine Careers

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion
We're committed to a safe, respectful and inclusive Nine. From day one, you'll be encouraged to bring your whole self to work and will be supported to perform at your best.

We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, people with disabilities, and of all ages, genders, nationalities, backgrounds and cultures as we recognise the importance and value of diverse perspectives. Should you require any adjustments to the recruitment process, please advise us when you apply.

Cross River Rail Delivery Authority (CRRDA)…Your chance to work with the best!

The Cross River Rail Delivery Authority is building a new 10.2km rail line that includes 5.9km of twin tunnels running under the Brisbane River and CBD; with four new underground stations at Boggo Road, Woolloongabba, Albert Street and Roma Street.

Once complete, it will provide trains with a much-needed alternative rail path through the core of the current SEQ Rail network, unlocking a bottleneck and allowing more trains to run more often across the whole of South East Queensland.

The Cross River Rail Delivery Authority is also delivering multiple supporting projects and activities in conjunction with the Department of Transport and Main Roads. These include accessibility rebuilds for eight surface stations, construction of three new stations on the Gold Coast; upgrades for stabling yards; track works and surface rail enhancements; and the introduction of a new ETCS digital signalling system.

 

About the Role

The Program Performance Insights Lead plays a critical role in helping senior leaders understand what is happening across a complex, integrated infrastructure program, why it matters, and what decisions or actions may be required at key points in the program lifecycle.

Working under the direction of the Performance Reporting and Improvement Manager, the role draws on deep delivery and program experience to interpret inputs from across delivery teams, Program Controls and assurance functions, identify what is material, and shape clear, credible narratives and insights used in executive and governance forums.

The role is closely connected to regular executive and governance reporting cycles, requiring active forward planning, judgement about timing, and ensuring issues and decisions flow coherently from one forum to the next. This includes anticipating upcoming reviews and decisions, ensuring material issues are surfaced early, and that outcomes and directions agreed through governance forums are accurately reflected in subsequent reporting.

This role suits someone who has worked inside complex project or program environments and now wants to apply that experience at an enterprise level. Influence is exercised through judgement, perspective and clarity, rather than through direct line authority or delivery ownership.

This opportunity is offered as a fixed term contract until August 2028 with possible extension.

 

Key Responsibilities:

Shape executive‑ready reporting narratives and recommendations that translate complex program performance into clear decision support
Translate program performance into insights that clearly link delivery status to risk, dependencies, commercial impacts and decision consequences.
Apply judgement to identify material issues, prioritise competing inputs and determine appropriate escalation pathways
Work closely with delivery teams, program controls and senior stakeholders to identify, test and refine reporting messages
Challenge inputs constructively to improve clarity, coherence and usefulness of reporting
Plan, manage and adapt integrated reporting and governance cadence so reporting aligns with decision making timelines
Ensure outcomes from governance forums are accurately reflected and tracked through subsequent reporting
Oversee the overall quality and presentation of reporting products, including the effective use of visuals and structure
Drive continuous improvement in reporting and governance processes to improve clarity, timeliness and reduce rework

 

About You:

Experience working in complex project or program environments, with a strong understanding of how delivery issues, risks and dependencies play out in practice
Strong ability to synthesise complex information and translate delivery reality into clear narratives, insights and advice for senior decision‑making
Sound judgement and the ability to make sense of complex or competing inputs in senior reporting or decision‑support contexts
Clear, confident communicator with the ability to engage and collaborate with senior stakeholders, delivery teams, project controls and other functions.
Organised and adaptable, with the ability to operate within regular executive and governance reporting cycles without losing attention to quality
Relevant tertiary qualifications or equivalent professional experience

 

Why work with CRRDA?

On Cross River Rail you will not only work with the best but become your best. And as we enter a new phase of delivery, our project offers a rare career opportunity for top tier professionals to have played their part on something that is genuinely city shaping and transformative.

Our people are supported with flexible work options, career development through the Australian Institute of Management, salary packaging and access to world class employee assistance provider Telus Health.

At Cross River Rail we are an equal opportunity employer and strive to build balanced teams from all walks of life.

 

How To Apply

Please submit your Resume and a short cover letter outlining your suitability referencing the competencies listed in the 'About You' section of this advertisement by clicking on the apply link before the closing date of 31st May 2026. 

Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people are strongly encouraged to apply for all Cross River Rail Delivery Authority vacancies.

Business Analyst

Large-scale transformation program

High profile workstream delivering significant uplifts at the heart of our business

12 month max-term contract - Melbourne location

About the Role

Due to significant global growth, Kinetic are undertaking a large scale, multi stream program of work designed to transform our technology landscape, create simplified, consistent ways of working, and ensure our future growth plans are underpinned by seamless technology products and processes. 

Our Business Analysts are responsible for analyzing business processes, identifying areas for improvement, and streamline solutions to enhance efficiency and effectiveness. This role requires strong analytical skills, excellent communication, and the ability to work collaboratively with various stakeholders.

This role will work closely on our Skybus ticketing uplift project - a high profile piece of work at the heart of the Kinetic business.

What You Will Do
Collaborate with project managers, IT and procurement teams to ensure alignment between business requirements and technical solutions.
Identify and document business requirements and translate them into functional specifications.
Collaborate with stakeholders to understand their needs and provide effective solutions.
Develop and maintain detailed project documentation, including artefacts, process flows, and user stories.
Facilitate meetings and workshops to gather requirements and present findings.
Assist in the development and implementation of new business processes and systems.
Monitor and evaluate the effectiveness of implemented solutions and make necessary adjustments.
Identify and record business benefits and link those to scope, and requirements.
Identify risks and issues and/or additional benefits from proposed solutions
Develop gap analysis and impact assessments where required
Support testing of solutions across the program of work
Develop and deliver business process mapping to support project and small initiative implementation (service delivery BAU initiatives)
Update and maintain project work items on JIRA and Confluence
What You Will Need
Experience working in ticketing or payments related projects would be highly advantageous
Proven experience in strategy analysis for business requirements, developing process maps and conducting gap-analysis and impact assessments for business improvement.
Experience supporting procurement processes and vendor evaluations
Ability to communicate with all stakeholders internal and external to the organisation.
Experience using Jira and Confluence.
Strong analytic skills with ability to develop and deliver business process-mapping to support project and small initiative implementation.
Previous experience working in complex multi-vendor environments.
Excellent communication skills and an approachable demeanor.
Proven ability in improving processes and driving change.
Self-motivated with a demonstrated enthusiastic approach to day-to-day business and positive approach to a continually changing work environment.
Most importantly, our values resonate with you – Passion, Integrity, Respect, Authenticity, Humility.
About us:

At Kinetic, we don't just move people - we're creating the positive change our planet needs. As a leader in sustainable transport, we're committed to creating cleaner, safer, and greener environments for generations to come.

Our size and scale give our people real opportunities to learn, grow, and progress in their careers. Calm, positive and respectful team players thrive here. Our people embrace change and are encouraged to stretch their comfort zone while making a difference. We challenge each other to be our best.

We're driven by respect, diversity and genuine connection. From networking groups to community partnerships and volunteering, our camaraderie naturally brings people together. Our energy and dedication keep communities moving and our people growing.

How to apply:

Kinetic is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer and our people represent the community which we service. We invite all suitably qualified applicants to apply, including First Nations People, and people from diverse social, cultural and gender backgrounds.

If you're interested in this rewarding role with Kinetic then click the 'Apply' button now!

About ElectraNet

ElectraNet builds, owns, operates and maintains the high voltage electricity transmission network which delivers the energy South Australians rely on to power our homes, businesses and communities. As an industry leader, we're at the forefront of the clean energy transition—socially and environmentally ambitious and committed to creating opportunities for people and nature to thrive. We're equally committed to a workplace culture where people feel safe, supported and empowered to speak up, manage risk, and learn and grow together.

The Opportunity

Join ElectraNet at a pivotal time as they lead South Australia's clean energy transition, stepping into a highly influential Data & AI Governance Lead role. This position offers the opportunity to design and embed a modern, decision centric governance model that shapes how data and AI are used across the enterprise.

You will work closely with senior leaders, technology teams and AI practitioners to drive clarity, trust and accountability in decision-making. The role is highly strategic, focused on enabling better business outcomes through governed data assets and responsible AI practices. It is ideal for someone who thrives on influencing at scale, balancing governance with innovation, and embedding lasting organisational change.

Establish and sustain data, AI and decision governance structures aligned to business objectives.
Clarify ownership, accountability and controls across data assets, AI models and automated decision systems.
Work across business and technology teams to shape governance practices and decision-making on AI usage.
Define governance expectations including oversight, monitoring, and accountability for AI models and agents.
Integrate governance into business-as-usual processes, enabling value rather than constraining delivery.

About You

You are a confident and pragmatic data or AI governance professional who thrives on influencing at the enterprise level and driving meaningful organisational change. With proven experience designing and embedding governance frameworks, you ensure governance enables rather than slows innovation.

You bring strong stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to partner with senior leaders and translate complex concepts into practical, business-ready approaches. Your expertise in data, AI models and decision frameworks allows you to build trust, accountability and transparency across the organisation.

Most importantly, you're passionate about shaping how data and AI are responsibly used to drive better decisions and outcomes.

Demonstrated experience designing and embedding governance frameworks and operating models.
Proven ability to engage and influence senior leaders across business and technology.
Ability to define governance, accountability and controls for AI models and agents.
Experience with enterprise data models, catalogues and governance artefacts.
Ability to design governance that enables outcomes, not just compliance.

Qualifications

Relevant tertiary qualification in information management, technology, data, engineering, governance or a related discipline, or equivalent experience.

Employee Benefits

We're proud to offer a workplace that is supportive, inclusive and genuinely committed to your success. When you join ElectraNet, you join a team that values diversity, collaboration and continuous learning.

We offer a competitive remuneration package starting at $160,900 plus superannuation and a performance bonus eligibility of up to 15%.

In addition to this, we also offer:

Ongoing professional development and certification support
A vibrant and community focused team culture
Regular social events and activities.

Job Summary:

We are seeking an experienced Principal Data Architect professional to lead end‑to‑end migration from legacy systems to modern data platforms. The role involves defining migration strategy and architecture, overseeing execution, ensuring data quality and reconciliation, and managing cutover with minimal business disruption. The candidate will drive governance, handle high‑volume mission‑critical data, and provide leadership across teams to ensure successful, compliant, and efficient data transformation.
 

Roles & Responsibilities:

Define the overall Data Migration Architecture for Mainframe modernization programs

Design scalable source‑to‑target data models, mapping legacy structures to modern platforms

Establish migration patterns (big‑bang, phased, wave‑based, or domain‑wise)

Drive data decomposition strategies for large and tightly coupled mainframe datasets

Ensure architecture aligns with performance, security, audit, and compliance requirements

Lead end‑to‑end data migration delivery across multiple pods / workstreams

Oversee data extraction, transformation, loading (ETL/ELT) from Mainframe sources

Govern batch sequencing, dependency management, restartability, and error handling

Ensure zero or minimal business disruption during migration cycles

Coordinate with application, testing, and infrastructure teams for integrated delivery

Define and enforce data validation frameworks, including:

Row counts & record balancing

Control totals and hash reconciliations

Referential integrity checks

Business rule validations

Own pre‑ and post‑migration reconciliation sign‑off

Drive defect triage and root cause analysis for data discrepancies

Ensure auditability and traceability across migration runs

Plan and execute mock migrations and dress rehearsals

Lead cutover planning, including rollback and contingency strategies

Support parallel runs and controlled transition from Mainframe to target systems

Work closely with business stakeholders to validate data correctness post go‑live

Establish migration governance frameworks and reporting mechanisms

Identify and mitigate data migration risks, including data loss, corruption, and performance bottlenecks

Provide regular status, risk, and dependency updates to senior stakeholders

Support client workshops on migration approach, risk posture, and readiness.

Mentor migration architects, data engineers, and testing teams

Drive best practices, accelerators, and reusable assets for Mainframe migration

Contribute to future‑state data strategy beyond migration (modernization, analytics, optimization)




Skills Required:

The role requires deep, hands‑on expertise in Mainframe Data Migration, including:

Legacy Mainframe systems (COBOL, JCL, DB2, VSAM, IMS) and their data structures

End‑to‑end legacy‑to‑target data migration covering extraction, transformation, validation, and reconciliation

Migration of high‑volume, mission‑critical telecom data with strict SLAs and regulatory requirements

Strong understanding of batch processing, file‑based data movement, and scheduling frameworks

Experience migrating data to modern data platforms (Cloud DW, Lakehouse, Analytics platforms)

Data quality, reconciliation, control totals, audit checks, and post‑migration assurance.

Managing cutover, dress rehearsals, and parallel runs in large transformation programs




Benefits:

Joining Cognizant will give you the opportunity to learn and collaborate with some of the most talented people in the industry, while having your finger on the pulse of emerging industry trends and working on the cutting edge of technology in your field of expertise.

We recognize that our people perform at their best when they feel valued as significant contributors and that is why at Cognizant, taking care of our employees is a priority:

Role purpose

The key purpose of the Senior Business Analyst is to play a strategic role in understanding business needs and enabling technological solutions that provide real business value. The Senior Business Analyst contributes to the achievement of the overall business strategy by providing insights and recommendations for technology supported solutions based on comprehensive analysis of organisational strategy, processes and systems, and data. This role collaborates with a broad range of stakeholders and multiple business units to enable them to understand the problem, define project outcomes, gather, analyse, validate requirements, and develop a project strategy.

Key Accountabilities


Stakeholder Engagement & Relationship Management    

Establish and maintain strong relationships with key stakeholders, enabling an in-depth understanding of cross-business functions, how they interrelate, and contribute to overall organisational strategy.

Collaborate with key business stakeholders to identify enterprise-wide opportunities and validate business value that aligns with the overall business strategy.

Communicate effectively with both technical and non-technical audiences to ensure a shared understanding of requirements, outcomes, and project status.




Business Analysis & Solution Development    

Tackle complex and high-impact business problems, offering innovative insights and achievable solutions, ensuring alignment with organisational goals and challenges.

Prepare, perform, and coordinate elicitation of requirements, including brainstorming, workshops, prototyping, observation, or interviews. Assess current state, synthesise results, and provide actionable insights for technology-supported solutions.

Create business personas and user stories, ensuring that business outcomes are captured and replayed to stakeholders for validation.

Work with technical teams to ensure that solution designs address business needs and outcomes and align with technology capabilities.


Documentation & Requirements Management    

Create detailed documentation for solution design, including business requirements, functional specifications, and process workflows.

Ensure documentation is clear, comprehensive, and aligned with organisational standards, supporting business and technical teams throughout the project lifecycle.


Project & Change Management    

Prepare business change and communication plans to support the implementation of technology solutions and process improvements.

Lead the gathering and documenting of User Acceptance Testing (UAT) requirements and participate in the review and validation of test cases to ensure they accurately represent the intended functionality.


Outcome Validation & Benefit Realisation    

Support business owners in defining outcomes, benefits, and measures of success, and verify and validate the value of improvements against these measures.


Leadership & Mentorship    

Provide leadership and mentorship to Business Analysts, guiding their development and ensuring alignment with best practices and organisational objectives.


Key Role Relationships

Functional Business Owners

Functional Team Leads

Functional SMEs

Technology Teams


Experience & Capabilities


ESSENTIAL EXPERIENCE (what you have done)    

Proven experience working with Salesforce CRM; exposure to financial systems is highly desirable

7 or more years of experience as a Senior Business Analyst or in a similar analytical role.

Strong working knowledge of Jira

Excellent stakeholder and relationship management skills with a demonstrated ability to work with a cross section of technology and business stakeholders.

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.

Demonstrated experience in delivering a broad range of Business Analyst artefacts.


CRITICAL KNOWLEDGE (what you need to know)    

Understanding of business strategy and organisational goals and challenges and how individual business units contribute.

Understanding and experience in both agile and waterfall methodologies and an ability to work across a blend of both dependent on project and business needs.


CAPABILITIES (what you can do)    

Ability to analyse organisation and departmental strategy, processes and systems, and data, current pain points and problems to be solved, and synthesise into actionable insights and recommendations that will drive business value.

Effective information gathering techniques to proactively uncover requirements and synthesise information accurately and coherently and provide actionable insights.

Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a team environment.

Ability to self-organise and meet deadlines.

Ability to lead and motivate teams including managing conflict.

An adaptable and experimentation enterprise mindset with a commitment to continuous improvement.

Familiarity with project management methodologies, tools, and techniques.


PERSONAL ATTRIBUTES (who you are)    
You consistently demonstrate and role model the behaviours that bring the Vicinity values to life: 


Respect: 

We listen to and acknowledge each other's views 

We have difficult conversations with care 


Integrity: 

We back our words with the right actions

We do the right thing, no matter the situation 


Customer Focus: 

We nurture a genuine connection with our customers 

We consider customer needs when making decisions 


Collaboration: 

We invite the right people to the table 

We balance consensus with decisive actions 


Excellence: 

We always strive to improve

We share our challenges and celebrate the wins
 

Why Vicinity?




Our benefits program focuses on creating an awesome place to work in which our people are rewarded and recognised. This includes:

Flexible working options 

Birthday leave & purchased additional leave

$1,000 worth of VCX securities rewarded for eligible team members

Internal mentoring program

Job Opportunity: Data Quality Manager

Role Type: Initial 12- 18 Month Contract

Location: Sydney CBD (Hybrid - Min 3 days in-office)

Excellent Daily Rate.


The Opportunity

We are seeking a Data Quality Manager for a high-profile, tech-enabled transformation. This role acts as a critical bridge between technical delivery streams and executive leadership. You will be responsible for translating complex data analysis into strategic insights for senior stakeholders, ensuring a "one best way" approach to our systems and data.

Key Responsibilities

Data Migration Leadership: Lead hands-on data quality activities, profiling, and validation specifically for large-scale migration milestones.

Technical Analysis: Utilize SQL to interrogate large datasets, grabbing raw analysis and shaping it into professional presentations for high-level decision-making.

Executive Interfacing: Act as a primary point of contact for GMs and GEs, presenting data findings and influencing remediation pathways.

Strategic Coordination: Bridge the gap between technical teams and senior stakeholders to ensure requirements are understood and issues are clearly articulated.

Autonomous Delivery: Operate with high autonomy to maintain momentum, manage dependencies, and ensure evidence is produced in line with governance standards.



Requirements

Experience: 6-8 years of experience in a Data Management or Data Quality Managerial role.

Technical Proficiency: Strong SQL skills and experience working directly with complex datasets.

Migration Expertise: Proven track record in large-scale data migration programs within financial services or regulated environments.

Stakeholder Management: Demonstrated experience talking to and influencing Senior Executives (GMs/GEs).

Problem Solving: A self-starter mindset with the ability to navigate complex and ambiguous delivery environments.

On-site Collaboration: Ability to work from the Sydney office 3-4 days per week for essential face-to-face workshops and team interaction.



The Ideal Candidate

You are a structured and collaborative leader who can "turn intent into execution". You excel at taking deep technical data and framing it in a way that resonates at the Board and General Manager level.
